```plaintext
Upon reviewing the literature again, we find detailed descriptions supporting our initial conclusions. Specifically, Vibrio spp. indeed thrive in hypoxic conditions similar to those required by true microaerophiles (<5% O2), making Vibrio a strong candidate. However, recent insights indicate Campylobacter species also show preferences for partially oxygen-reduced atmospheres—approximately 5-10% O2—which matches precisely what defines microaerophilism. Therefore, re-examining key references reveals compelling support for \textbf{Campylobacter}, reinforcing why it stands out among listed options given its precise oxygen usage pattern compared to alternatives like Vibrio or Bacteroides.
